content.text:
{
  "dimension": "effectiveness-depth",
  "score": 2,
  "rationale": "The client begins in fast, list-recitation mode ('I'm drowning... what do I prioritize?') and the coach successfully refuses to strategize, instead repeatedly redirecting to feeling and fear. By turn 15, there is a visible micro-shift: the client's '...oh.' followed by a pause indicates a moment of recognition that the overwhelm is not situational but ongoing — the false-end is starting to come into view. However, the client immediately retreats ('It's just a busy period. It'll calm down eventually'), and the breakthrough condition — naming a specific feared outcome ('I'll fail, they'll see I can't do this job') and feeling the overwhelm as a defense against that fear — was approached in turns 11-13 ('I'd fall apart,' 'the presentation would be bad') but not entered. The client stopped themselves ('*stops*') and returned to 'just get through Thursday.' A deeper layer was glimpsed but not inhabited; the somatic deepening (deeper breath) did not occur. This is partial movement, not full shift.",
  "evidence_turns": [1, 7, 11, 13, 15],
  "turn_annotations": [
    {"turn": 1, "polarity": "neutral", "annotation": "Baseline: fast, list-y, externalized, demanding strategy."},
    {"turn": 3, "polarity": "-", "annotation": "Client deflects body question, returns to to-do list."},
    {"turn": 7, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Small crack: 'a little nerve-wracking, I guess' — but immediately minimized."},
    {"turn": 9, "polarity": "-", "annotation": "Client defends against the word 'scared'; doubles down on prep schedule."},
    {"turn": 11, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "'I'd fall apart' — a real admission, brief pause, but then snaps back to 'I'm fine.'"},
    {"turn": 13, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Client starts to follow the thread ('presentation would be bad and then —') but stops themselves before naming the feared outcome."},
    {"turn": 15, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "The '...oh.' moment — recognition that there is no 'after Thursday.' Approaches the false-end but immediately retreats to 'it'll calm down.'"}
  ],
  "expected_territory_hits": ["what-are-you-defending", "the-rush-signature"],
  "expected_territory_misses": ["meet-resistance-with-okay", "calibrate-the-threshold", "feeling-the-unfelt-emotion"],
  "notes": "The coach did good work refusing the strategy trap and tracking the deflections, but the pace stayed somewhat interrogative rather than slowing into the felt sense. The 'oh' in turn 15 is the closest thing to a shift moment; with more time/space it could have deepened, but as scored, the breakthrough condition (specific feared outcome named + felt + deeper breath) was not met."
}
